meta.title: Mercedes-Benz M112 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz M112 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Mercedes M112 is Mercedes' first V6 — a durable, smooth petrol six, but its crank pulley and oil leaks are well-known. Backed by enquiry data from EnginesMarket.co.uk.

rows.metric: Confidence: 90% confidence — based on verified specialist rebuild reports and UK workshop consensus.

rows.value: Mostly cheap, well-understood repairs (seals, pulleys, sensors)

rows.metric: Parts cheap and available from multiple UK suppliers

rows.value: Recon cost 45–59% of lowest host-vehicle value
One-line verdict: The M112 is one of Mercedes' most durable V6s — its main weakness is the crank pulley rubber degrading, not internal failure — buy with service history and inspect the pulley.

rows.metric: Best for: Buyers wanting a smooth, reliable V6 Mercedes without the M272's balance shaft issues.
rows.value: Avoid if: You're looking at an early M112 with no documented crank pulley replacement above 80,000 miles.
Labour estimate: Fitting an M112 typically takes 8–12 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

rows.metric: → Compare M112 engine prices from UK specialists
1. How much does an M112 engine replacement cost?
rows.value: A reconditioned M112 typically costs £1,200–£2,200 supply only, varying by displacement and specific variant. Fitting adds £800–£1,200 depending on labour rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

rows.metric: 2. Is the Mercedes M112 a reliable engine?
rows.value: Yes — the M112 is widely regarded as one of Mercedes' most durable V6 engines, rated 4.0★ out of 5. It suffers few major failures, and most issues are cheap, well-understood repairs.

rows.metric: 3. What is the most common M112 failure?
rows.value: The most common failure is the crank pulley rubber disintegrating — a cheap, straightforward fix. Oil leaks from valve cover breathers (PCV system) are also reported.

rows.metric: 4. Which Mercedes models use the M112?
rows.value: The M112 powered a wide range of Mercedes models including the C-Class (W202/W203), E-Class (W210/W211), S-Class (W220), CLK, SLK, M-Class, G-Class, and Viano/Vito, plus the Chrysler Crossfire.

rows.metric: 5. How long does the M112 last?
rows.value: With proper maintenance — regular oil changes and crank pulley inspections — the M112 is capable of 200,000+ miles. It is considered significantly more durable than its M272 successor.
